Title: Functional expression of the nitrogenase Fe protein in transgenic rice

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-expression of nifM enabled soluble accumulation and maturation of mitochondrial NifH in transgenic rice, indirectly supporting partial Fe protein activity relevant to biological nitrogen fixation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable co-expression in rice; qRT-PCR; immunoblotting/indirect evidence from NifH solubility; purification of NifH; acetylene reduction assay with NifDK; FeMo-co synthesis assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Azotobacter vinelandii nifM was co-expressed with Hydrogenobacter thermophilus nifH in rice mitochondria. 2. Although NifM protein was not clearly detected in plant extracts, soluble OsNifHHt accumulation indicated sufficient NifM function for NifH folding/maturation. 3. This support allowed recovery of partially active NifH from rice tissues.
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: protein folding/maturation of nitrogenase Fe protein
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsActin promoter (constitutive) with Su9 mitochondrial transit peptide
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in rice callus and regenerated plants, with higher mRNA levels in plants than callus
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Harvest before light onset at end of dark period; Fe fertilization with 100 μM Fe(III)-EDDHA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory gene for engineering nitrogenase in cereals because it improves NifH folding/solubility in mitochondria; likely should be stacked with nifH and future Fe-S delivery solutions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: nifM is a core experimental gene in Results because it was co-expressed as the minimal accessory factor required to obtain soluble NifH, but its effect is indirect and no direct metabolite accumulation in rice was measured.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Nitrogenase iron protein / dinitrogenase reductase component
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression of mitochondrial nifH in rice produced soluble Fe protein with partial activity in electron transfer to NifDK and in FeMo-co biosynthesis, representing a key step toward engineering biological nitrogen fixation in cereals.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers electrons from the reduced Fe protein to the MoFe protein in an ATP-dependent manner during nitrogenase catalysis; also functions in maturation of nitrogenase cofactors including FeMo-co.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ATP; electrons delivered to NifH; NifDK/NifEN-associated maturation systems
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced MoFe protein during catalytic cycle; support of FeMo-co maturation rather than a simple small-molecule product
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core nitrogenase catalytic electron-transfer module and FeMo-co maturation module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream electron-donor component essential for catalytic reduction of N2 and cofactor maturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for nitrogenase function and supports eventual ammonia formation; also required for FeMo-co biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point not formally established as rate-limiting; activity in planta was limited by poor [4Fe-4S] cluster occupancy
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in rice callus and regenerated plants; higher mRNA levels in plants than callus; stably inherited in T1 progeny
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter plus first intron, ZmUbi1+1st intron (constitutive), with Cox4 mitochondrial transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fe fertilization with 100 μM Fe(III)-EDDHA; tissue harvested before light onset at end of dark period to minimize O2 damage; anaerobic purification and assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression in rice; qRT-PCR; immunoblotting; Strep-tag affinity purification; N-terminal sequencing; mass spectrometry; acetylene reduction assay with purified A. vinelandii NifDK; in vitro [4Fe-4S] reconstitution with EcNifU; in vitro FeMo-co synthesis assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Hydrogenobacter thermophilus nifH targeted to rice mitochondria accumulated as a soluble, processed protein in callus and plants. 2. Purified OsNifHHt showed low but definite Fe protein activity in acetylene reduction assays when combined with A. vinelandii NifDK. 3. In vitro reconstitution with [4Fe-4S]-loaded NifU increased activity about 9-fold, indicating incomplete cluster occupancy in rice mitochondria. 4. Purified OsNifHHt also supported NifB-dependent in vitro FeMo-co synthesis, confirming a second fundamental NifH function. 5. The work establishes functional expression of a nitrogenase structural component in rice.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value foundational gene for engineering biological nitrogen fixation in cereals; should be stacked with accessory genes and host modifications that improve mitochondrial Fe-S cluster insertion, Fe homeostasis, electron transfer, and O2 protection.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Current activity is low because much protein accumulates as apo-NifH with poor [4Fe-4S] occupancy; insufficient active protein may limit utility until Fe-S delivery and energetics are improved.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species and applied species differ: nifH derives from H. thermophilus but function was tested in transgenic O. sativa using heterologous A. vinelandii NifDK and FeMo-co synthesis components. The study did not measure in planta ammonia production directly.
